file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1168.lean` — Erdős Problem 1168

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1168`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/1168](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1168)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (negative partition relation without GCH).
- [x] Bibliography entry provided: `[Va99]` Vaughan (1999).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1168 ... end Erdos1168`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1168`.
- [x] `open Cardinal Ordinal` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1168` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 3 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the partition relation and its conditions.
- [x] Docstring references the source: `[Va99, 7.80]`.

### `answer()` usage

The module docstring describes a problem asserting a negative partition relation holds (without GCH). This is **Case 3** — a direct assertion.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) — correct for a direct assertion.
- [x] Quantifiers are theorem-body-level (inside the statement), which is fine since no `answer()` is used.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 3 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 3 and 5).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: 3, 5.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 3 (Mathematical logic and foundations) and 5 (Combinatorics) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` followed by `sorry` on the next line (acceptable multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants present. Only the main theorem `erdos_1168`.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≠`, `¬`, `∧`, `ℕ`, `ℵ_`, `ω`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter passes not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant with the style checklist. The formalization correctly captures the negative partition relation $\aleph_{\omega+1} \nrightarrow (\aleph_{\omega+1}, 3, \ldots, 3)_{\aleph_0}^2$ by encoding unordered-pair coloring as a symmetric function `c : α → α → ℕ` with the two key conditions: no monochromatic set of size $\aleph_{\omega+1}$ for color 0, and no monochromatic triple for any color $n \geq 1$. No issues found.

content.text: The review result is **PASS**. No changes needed.
